segments.text: Where has your beloved gone, O fairest among women? Where has your beloved turned aside, That we may seek him with you?
segments.speaker: Chorus or Friends

segments.text: My beloved has gone to his garden, To the beds of spices, To feed his flock in the gardens, And to gather lilies.
segments.speaker: The Shulamite

segments.text: I am my beloved's, And my beloved is mine. He feeds his flock among the lilies.
segments.speaker: The Shulamite

segments.text: O my love, you are as beautiful as Tirzah, Lovely as Jerusalem, Awesome as an army with banners!
segments.speaker: The Beloved

segments.text: Turn your eyes away from me, For they have overcome me. Your hair is like a flock of goats Going down from Gilead.
segments.speaker: The Beloved

segments.text: Your teeth are like a flock of sheep Which have come up from the washing; Every one bears twins, And none is barren among them.
segments.speaker: The Beloved

segments.text: Like a piece of pomegranate Are your temples behind your veil.
segments.speaker: The Beloved

segments.text: There are sixty queens And eighty concubines, And virgins without number.

My dove, my perfect one, Is the only one, The only one of her mother, The favorite of the one who bore her. The daughters saw her And called her blessed, The queens and the concubines, And they praised her.
segments.speaker: The Beloved

segments.text: Who is she who looks forth as the morning, Fair as the moon, Clear as the sun, Awesome as an army with banners?
segments.speaker: Chorus or Friends

segments.text: I went down to the garden of nuts To see the verdure of the valley, To see whether the vine had budded And the pomegranates had bloomed.
segments.speaker: The Shulamite

segments.text: Before I was even aware, My soul had made me As the chariots of my noble people.
segments.speaker: The Beloved

segments.text: Return, return, O Shulamite; Return, return, that we may look upon you! What would you see in the Shulamite— As it were, the dance of the two camps?
segments.speaker: Chorus or Friends
